2605.00696.title: Adaptive Querying with AI Persona Priors
2605.00696.paper_summary: We study adaptive querying for learning user-dependent quantities of interest, such as responses to held-out items and psychometric indicators, within tight question budgets. Classical Bayesian design and computerized adaptive testing typically rely on restrictive parametric assumptions or expensive posterior approximations, limiting their use in heterogeneous, high-dimensional, and cold-start settings. We introduce a persona-induced latent variable model that represents a user's state through membership in a finite dictionary of AI personas, each offering response distributions produced by a large language model. This yields expressive priors with closed-form posterior updates and efficient finite-mixture predictions, enabling scalable Bayesian design for sequential item selection. Experiments on synthetic data and WorldValuesBench demonstrate that persona-based posteriors deliver accurate probabilistic predictions and an interpretable adaptive elicitation pipeline.
2605.00696.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究在緊湊的問題預算內，針對學習用戶依賴的興趣量進行自適應查詢，例如對保留項目的反應和心理測量指標。傳統的貝葉斯設計和計算機自適應測試通常依賴於限制性的參數假設或昂貴的後驗近似，這限制了它們在異質性、高維度和冷啟動環境中的使用。我們引入了一種基於角色的潛在變量模型，通過在有限的 AI 角色字典中的成員身份來表示用戶的狀態，每個角色提供由大型語言模型生成的反應分佈。這產生了具有封閉形式後驗更新的表達性先驗和高效的有限混合預測，使得可擴展的貝葉斯設計成為可能，以進行序列項目選擇。在合成數據和 WorldValuesBench 上的實驗表明，基於角色的後驗提供了準確的概率預測和可解釋的自適應引導流程。
2605.00696.author: Kaizheng Wang et.al.
2605.00696.authors: Kaizheng Wang, Yuhang Wu, Assaf Zeevi
